text.title: django-serializers Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for DRF serializer design. Each is checkable by AST inspection or a code-review checklist.

rule.statement: Every endpoint that both accepts data and returns data MUST have separate Input (`EntityCreateRequest`, `EntityUpdateRequest`) and Output (`EntityResponse`, `EntityListResponse`) serializers. Both MUST inherit from `serializers.Serializer`. Forbid `ModelSerializer` with `fields = "__all__"`. Forbid one serializer used for both directions.
rule.rationale: A single shared serializer paired with `__all__` leaks every new model field into the response automatically. Splitting the directions makes the API surface auditable and stable against model changes.

rule.statement: `validate_*()` and `validate()` methods MUST do shape/format checks only — no ORM queries, no service calls, no model creation/mutation. Cross-field business rules (uniqueness, ownership, side-effects) belong in `services.py`.
rule.rationale: Database access in validate() runs outside the service transaction; a service rollback leaves orphan state. Business logic in validate() bypasses the unit-testable service layer and conflates the validation step with the action step.

rule.statement: Output serializers MUST list every exposed field explicitly. Forbid `fields = "__all__"`, forbid `exclude = [...]` as the security mechanism. Sensitive fields (password, secret, token, api_key, is_staff, internal_*) MUST be absent from the output serializer's field list.
rule.rationale: An explicit allowlist is safe-by-default when a model gains a new field; an excludelist requires every author to remember the rule. Empirically, secrets leak via missed exclude entries in every codebase audited.

rule.statement: Every Input serializer field MUST set `required`, `allow_blank`, `allow_null`, and (when applicable) `default` explicitly. Use `write_only=True` for password / secret fields on Input. Use `read_only=True` for derived fields on Output. Do not rely on implicit DRF defaults.
rule.rationale: DRF defaults change between major versions and depend on field type. Explicit options make the API contract visible in source and stable across upgrades.

rule.statement: Nested serializers MUST consume pre-fetched data provided by a selector (via `select_related` / `prefetch_related`). Forbid `source="...all"` or `PrimaryKeyRelatedField(queryset=...)` patterns that trigger ad-hoc queries during render. Use `is_valid(raise_exception=True)` in views and pass `serializer.validated_data` to the service.
rule.rationale: A nested serializer that triggers its own query reintroduces N+1 inside the response phase, invisible to selector-level N+1 tests. `raise_exception=True` standardises 400 responses across endpoints.
